features.featureTitle: Replace all existing dashboard components with a single clean Top100Dashboard component. Fetch the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market cap from the CoinGecko public API (endpoint: /api/v3/coins/markets, params: vs_currency=usd, order=market_cap_desc, per_page=100, page=1, sparkline=false). Display results in a paginated, sortable table with columns: Rank, Name/Symbol (with coin logo), Price (USD), 24h Change (%), Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio (24h volume ÷ market cap × 100, as a percentage), and Fully Diluted Market Cap. Update App.tsx to render only the new Top100Dashboard.

Add a filter and sort control bar above the Top 100 table. Include: a text search input to filter coins by name or symbol; four dropdown selectors to sort/filter by Live Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio, and Fully Diluted Market Cap (each with High→Low and Low→High options); and a Reset button that clears all active filters and search input, restoring the default market-cap-desc order.

Add a dedicated Price Projections section below the Top 100 table. The section must include: a searchable coin selector dropdown populated from the 100 fetched coins; a 12-month rolling projection panel displayed once a coin is selected, showing a monthly table (Month 1–12 from today) with low, mid, and high projected prices and a confidence score per month; and a line chart (using Recharts) visualising the mid projected price trajectory across all 12 months. Projections must be computed entirely on the frontend using the selected coin's current price, 30-day historical price data fetched from CoinGecko (/api/v3/coins/{id}/market_chart?vs_currency=usd&days=30), and linear regression + volatility logic (reuse or adapt frontend/src/utils/projections.ts).

Implement a clean React Query data-fetching layer in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Provide a useTopCoins hook (fetches the top 100 coins market data with a 60-second stale time and in-memory cache) and a useCoinHistory hook (fetches 30-day price history for a given coin ID, with a 5-minute stale time). Both hooks must handle loading, error, and empty states, fall back to cached data on API errors, and include rate-limit-safe retry logic (exponential backoff, max 3 retries).

Apply a cohesive dark-mode-first visual theme across the entire rebuilt application. Use a near-black charcoal base (#0d0f14 or equivalent), neon cyan primary accent, and gold secondary accent. Use a clean sans-serif typeface. The sticky header must display 'Crypto Tracker AI' branding with a live indicator badge. Tables, cards, and dropdowns must use subtle glassmorphism / frosted-panel styling. Positive 24h price changes render in emerald green, negative in red. The full layout must be responsive down to 375 px mobile width.
